resources.string:
Francesco Andreuzzi (Italy)
	-> Programmer 

Thanks to Luke Winward for his original idea
Refresh: apps, alias, music, contacts, rss feeds
Lock the screen on double tap
Re:T-UI is busy, use \"ctrlc\" to stop the process
Do you like my work? You can offer me a coffee (cmd: $ donate). Thank you!
You can\'t perform a manual update with the default key. You have to create a custom key on the website
The weather API is busy. Trying again at:
The index is greater than the shortcuts count
No bound app found with ID:
You don\'t have an old theme
Unfortunately Re:T-UI can\'t redirect you to the \"Notification access\" page of your system settings
Notifications are supported on Android 4.3+ only. Use \'config -set show_notifications false\' to disable this message

First time with Re:T-UI?
	help -> command list and workstation quickstart
	guide -> non-blocking walkthrough
	tutorial -> open the wiki
You have to grant Storage permission
Your apps preferences couldn\'t be loaded because your \"Re:T-UI\" folder is not readable at the moment (you should try to use the command \"restart\")
Your rss preferences couldn\'t be loaded because your \"Re:T-UI\" folder is not readable at the moment (you should try to use the command \"restart\")
Your notifications preferences couldn\'t be loaded because your \"Re:T-UI\" folder is not readable at the moment (you should try to use the command \"restart\")
Your xml preferences couldn\'t be loaded because your \"Re:T-UI\" folder is not readable at the moment (you should try to use the command \"restart\")

string-array.name: hints
string-array.item: Need the map? \"$ help\" shows the command list and workstation quickstart
Did you know that you can save and switch your current look? Have a look at the cmd \"$ preset\"
Did you know? After \"$ wallpaper -auto\", you can lock in the result with \"$ preset -save mylook\"
Doubts about a command? Use \"$ help cmdName\"
Did you know? You can listen to music using Re:T-UI. Have a look at the cmd \"$ music\"
Did you know? Re:T-UI 6.4 introduced App Groups.\nYou can get more details with the cmd \"$ apps -tutorial\"
Did you know? You can see your notifications inside Re:T-UI. If you\'re interested, have a look at the tutorial: \"$ notifications -tutorial\"
Are you sick of clearing your screen? Have a look at the options \"clear_after_cmds\", \"clear_after_seconds\" and \"maxlines\"
Do you want to use your wallpaper as Re:T-UI background? Use the cmd: \"$ config -set system_wallpaper true\"
Doubts about any config option? Use the cmd \"$ config -info [option]\"table
Did you know that Re:T-UI supports aliases? For more info, use the cmd \"$ alias -tutorial\"
Did you know? You can exclude an unwanted notification by long-clicking on it
You can disable this messages with the cmd \"$ config -set show_hints false\"

string-array.name: tutorial
string-array.item: Use \"help\" for the command list and workstation quickstart
If you want details for one command, type \"help cmdName\"
Launch your apps typing their name
You can call your contacts with the cmd \"call contactName\"
Re:T-UI is highly customizable. Check \"config -ls\" or use the cmd \"config -tutorial\" to see what you can change
You can save your current colors with \"preset -save [name]\" and restore them with \"preset -apply [name]\"

resources.string: Re:T-UI will be closed in some seconds. Click the home button to restart it
There was a problem while connecting to your device camera
There was an XML problem with your file
, Re:T-UI will use default values.\nFix or delete the file to recover your options
Spaces are not allowed inside group names
The notification service couldn\'t be started:
Your notes reached the value set in notes_max_lines. You can scroll to see them all, change the value, or disable this message (ui.xml: show_scroll_notes_message).
Invalid ID: no RSS feed found
This RSS feed hasn\'t been checked yet
The time format couldn\'t be parsed
Invalid RSS: the file is empty:
There was a problem with your alias
is equal to its value. This may lead to bad behaviors, so it has been excluded. You should edit the name to use it

is equal to the part of its value before the first space (i.e. aliasName=play ; aliasValue = play store). This may lead to crashes and freezes, so it has been excluded. You should edit the name to use it

Print the list of commands, or info about a command 

Usage: 
help [command] 

Example: 
$ help exit
This file is too big, consider opening it with an other text editor
Bluetooth is not available on this device
Command not found.\nTry to use \"$ help\" or \"$ tutorial\"

Open the Re:T-UI Termux console.

Usage:
termux
termux -status
termux -setup
termux -open
termux -run <script_path> [args...]
termux -apps
termux -app <id>
termux -app-add <id> <command>
termux -app-info <id>
termux -app-sync <id>
termux -app-actions <id>
termux -app-action <id> <label> [input]
termux -app-action-rm <id> <label>

Inside the console:
status, setup, open, run, apps, app, app-add, app-info, app-sync, app-actions, app-action, app-action-rm, app-rm, help, clear, exit

For script modules:
module -add server termux:/data/data/com.termux/files/home/retui/server-health.sh
module -refresh server

Open Re:T-UI Files. 
Usage: 
$ files 
$ files <command> 

Launches the Re:T-UI Files app with the current path, theme colors, terminal background, top margin, and font.

Use Termux as Re:T-UI's power-user bridge for scripts, modules, callbacks, and automation. 
Usage: 
tbridge -status 
tbridge -doctor 
tbridge -setup 
tbridge -probe 

For file navigation, use: 
files 

For bridge-backed quick file actions, use ls/open/share with file_backend=termux. 

This bridge requires Termux, RUN_COMMAND permission, allow-external-apps=true, and termux-setup-storage.

Manage the Re:T-UI callback token for Termux, Tasker, and future modules. 

Usage: 
$ retui-token -status 
$ retui-token -show 
$ retui-token -rotate 
$ retui-token -on 
$ retui-token -off 

Callback action: 
com.dvil.tui_renewed.RETUI_CALLBACK 

Extras: 
token, action, text, title, module 

Safe actions now: 
output, notify, module_set 

Termux callback example: 
am broadcast -p com.dvil.tui_renewed -a com.dvil.tui_renewed.RETUI_CALLBACK --es token TOKEN --es action module_set --es module server --es text ONLINE

Show and manage Re:T-UI modules. 

Usage: 
$ module -ls 
$ module -show music 
$ module -show notifications 
$ module -show timer 
$ module -show calendar 
$ module -show reminder 
$ module -show notes 
$ module -show rss 
$ module -show weather 
$ module -prompt reminder add 
$ module -prompt reminder edit 
$ module -prompt reminder remove 
$ module -new lua counter 
$ module -edit counter 
$ module -config counter 
$ module -check counter 
$ module -approve counter 
$ module -export counter 
$ module -add server termux:/data/data/com.termux/files/home/retui/server-health.sh 
$ module -add events termux:/data/data/com.termux/files/home/retui/events.sh 
$ module -refresh server 
$ module -rm server 
$ module -hide music 
$ module -dock add server 
$ module -dock remove music 
$ module -close 

Modules are the single dock/panel category in Re:T-UI. A module can be native, Termux/shell-backed, launcher-backed, or Lua-backed. Built-in modules include music, notifications, timer, calendar, reminder, notes, rss, and weather. Lua modules are edited locally in Re:T-UI, can render native buttons/layouts, and use module -new lua, module -edit, module -config, module -check, and module -approve. Termux modules execute shell scripts and render ::title, ::body, and ::suggest metadata. Suggestion-only Lua scripts remain helpers because they do not render a dock panel. 

An empty dock stays empty. To hide the dock row entirely, turn off show_module_dock in Behavior settings or run config -set show_module_dock false.

Legacy aliases for Lua modules. Prefer the module command for new work. 

Usage: 
$ widget -ls 
$ widget -add counter 
$ widget -new counter 
$ widget -edit counter 
$ widget -config counter 
$ widget -rename counter better_counter 
$ widget -show counter 
$ widget -refresh counter 
$ widget -check counter 
$ widget -info counter 
$ widget -approve counter 
$ widget -copy-error counter 
$ widget -disable counter 
$ widget -enable counter 
$ widget -export counter 
$ widget -toggle counter 
$ widget -expand counter 
$ widget -collapse counter 
$ widget -rm counter 

The public category is module. Use module -new lua [name], module -edit [id], module -config [id], module -show [id], module -refresh [id], module -check [id], module -approve [id], and module -export [id]. Existing widget commands remain for compatibility with saved packages and older docs. Lua modules are stored locally under Re:T-UI's widgets folder for backup/restore compatibility, can register as lua:[id] modules in the shared dock, and support ui, prefs, files, json, date, fmt, strings, colors, debug, async http, config forms, expandable state, native buttons, command/action/dialog chips, app/intent/shortcut buttons, notification reminders, active ticking, suggestion scripts, system helpers, export, on_load/on_resume/on_alarm/on_tick/on_click/on_action/on_dialog_action/on_config/on_config_submit/on_suggest, and backup/restore.
write your SMS or nothing to cancel

-settings -> inspect runtime settings snapshot, notifications, music, fonts, and colors \n-theme -> inspect effective theme, auto color, and font state \n-presets -> inspect preset directory and available presets

Open the Re:T-UI settings/Themer overlay. 

Usage: 
$ settings

-static -> open the static wallpaper picker 
-live -> open the live wallpaper picker 
-auto -> enable or refresh wallpaper-derived theme colors 
(no args) -> open the wallpaper picker

-save [name] -> save the current theme.xml and suggestions.xml colors as a preset 
-apply [name] -> apply a saved or built-in preset and disable auto_color_pick 
-ls -> list saved and built-in presets 
To share a look, use Settings > System & Support > Create Shareable Configuration

-add [aliasName] [alias content] -> add a home alias 
-add -a [aliasName] [alias content] -> add a home alias 
-add -s [aliasName] [script path] -> add a Termux script alias 
-rm [aliasName] -> remove an existing alias 
-ls -> list your aliases 
-file -> open alias.txt

-ls -> open the app drawer, or list visible apps when the drawer is unavailable 
-lsh -> list your hidden apps 
-show [appName] -> re-show an hidden app 
-hide [appName] -> hide an app 
-l [appName] -> show details about an app 
-ps [appName] -> show Google Play Store page for an app 
-st [appName] -> show Settings details for an app 
-default_app [index] [appName or most_used or null] -> set the default_app at the specified index 
-frc [appName] -> force Re:T-UI to launch an app 
-file -> open apps.xml 
-reset [appName] -> reset the "launched_times" value of the given app to 0 
-mkgp [groupName] -> create a new group with the given name 
-rmgp [groupName] -> delete the group with the given name 
-gp_bg_color [groupName] [color] -> set the background color for the given group 
-gp_fore_color [groupName] [color] -> set the text color for the given group 
-addtogp [groupName] [appName] -> add the given app to the given group 
-rmfromgp [groupName] [appName] -> remove the given app from the given group 
-lsgp [OPTIONAL: groupName] -> list your groups, or list the content of the given group 
-tutorial -> open the Apps command guide
Toggle Bluetooth on older Android versions, or open Bluetooth settings on modern Android versions where direct toggles are restricted.
Set the brightness of your device \n$ brightness [0-100]

Perform basic operations (+ - * / % ^ sqrt) 
Example: 

$ calc 4+2-5 
1 

$ calc 2 ^ (2*2) 
16 

$ calc sqrt25 
5
Show the changelog of the current version

-set [option] [value] -> set the value of an option 
-append [option] [value] -> append the given string to the value of the selected option 
-file [file] -> edit a config file in the document editor (behavior.xml, ui.xml, theme.xml, ...) 
-erase [option] -> set the value of an option to "" 
-setr [option] [value] -> same to "-set", but restarts Re:T-UI automatically 
-get [option] -> get the value of an option 
-fontsize [value] -> change the global font size 
-reset [option] -> reset the value of an option 
-info [option] -> request info about the given option 
-apply [file] -> move a file to the Re:T-UI directory 
-tutorial -> open the tutorial page

Dev utilities. 
$ devutils -notify title body 
$ devutils -notify_reply title body 
$ devutils -check_notifications
Offer a coffee to the developer (PayPal only)

Manually switch the Surface Duo landscape companion layout. 
Requires behavior.xml duo_mode=true. 
$ duo status 
$ duo on 
$ duo off 
$ duo left 
$ duo right 
$ duo toggle 
Use aliases such as: 
$ alias -add duo-on duo on 
$ alias -add duo-off duo off
Play a fake hacking overlay animation

Print the available commands, or info about a command 

Usage: 
$ help [command]

Run a non-blocking command-first walkthrough 

Usage: 
$ guide 
$ guide -start basics 
$ guide -start customize 
$ guide -start modules 
$ guide -resume 
$ guide -restart basics 
$ guide -next 
$ guide -back 
$ guide -status 
$ guide -off 
$ guide -reset 

When a guide is active, the suggestion row stays locked to the current step command plus guide controls until guide -off. Progress is saved so guide -resume returns to the saved path and step.

Re:T-UI workstation quickstart 
This launcher expects you to drive. Use commands first; UI surfaces are helpers. 
 
Try: 
 guide -> start a non-blocking walkthrough 
 app name -> launch an app by name 
 alias -add ll apps -ls -> create a reusable shortcut 
 apps -hide appName -> remove visual noise from the drawer 
 wallpaper -auto -> derive a theme from your current wallpaper 
 preset -save mylook -> save the result 
 module -ls -> inspect available terminal modules 
 
Wiki: 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/Getting-Started 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/Command-Reference 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/Aliases 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/App-Drawer 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/Presets-and-Wallpaper-Auto-Color 
 https://github.com/DvilSpawn/Re-TUI/wiki/Termux-Integration

Extract text from HTML pages 
-query [filter ID] [optional: format ID] [URL] -> parse the specified XPath entry with the specified URL 
-add [type] [ID] [expression] -> add a new expression of the given type (json, xpath or format) 
-rm [ID] -> remove an existing expression 
-edit [ID] [expression] -> change an existing expression 
-ls [type] -> shows the expressions of the given type 
-tutorial -> show the tutorial on GitHub 
-file -> open the file htmlextract.xml

Save the Re:T-UI landscape preference and enable adaptive landscape behavior when the window is landscape. 

Usage: 
$ landscape 

Shortcut for orientation landscape.

Save the Re:T-UI portrait preference while Android controls the current window orientation. 

Usage: 
$ portrait 

Shortcut for orientation portrait.

Set Re:T-UI orientation preference. 

Usage: 
$ orientation portrait 
$ orientation landscape 
$ orientation auto 

Android controls the current window orientation. Re:T-UI adapts its layout when the device or window rotates.

-next -> play the next song 
-previous -> play the previous song 
-play -> play/pause the player 
-stop -> stop the player 
-ls -> list your tracks 
-select [song] -> play the selected song 
-info -> get info about the current song 
-seekto [seconds] -> seek to the selected part of the current song

Open or update notes. 

Usage: 
$ notes 
$ notes -add TODO: follow up 
$ notes -ls 
$ notes -rm [index or text prefix] 
$ notes -lock [index or text prefix] [true|false] 
$ notes -cp [index or text prefix] 
$ notes -clear 
$ notes -file 

The notes module treats TODO: notes as lightweight tasks without adding a separate task store.
Leave a feedback on the Play Store page
-add [ID] [regex] -> add a new regex with the given ID \n-rm [ID] -> remove the regex with the given ID \n-test [ID] [string] -> test the corresponding regex over the given string

-to [ID or packageName] [what] -> reply to the last notification from the application bound with the given ID 
-bind [appName] -> bind the application 
-unbind [appName] -> unbind the application 
-ls -> show your bound applications 
-check [ID or packageName] -> check the last notification from the application bound with the given ID 
-tutorial -> open the tutorial page on GitHub
Restart Re:T-UI and load modified values

Use a file-backed search provider before entering a query. 

-[provider] [search this...] -> use a provider from search.txt 
-add [provider] [url_template] -> add or replace a provider 
-rm [provider] -> remove a provider 
-ls -> list providers 
-file -> open search.txt 
-reset -> restore the default providers 

Template tokens: {query}, {query+}, {slug}, {raw}, {url} 
Example: search -add sdw https://stardewvalleywiki.com/{slug}

-ls [appName] -> show the shortcuts of this app 
-use [shortcutID OR shortcut index] [optional: appName] -> use the specified shortcut 
-use @alias -> use a pinned shortcut accepted by Re:T-UI 
-cp [index] [appName] -> copy to clipboard the nth shortcut of the selected app (first: 0)
Get info about battery charge, wifi status and mobile data

-apply [themeName] -> apply a shared upstream theme by ID 
-set [element] [color] -> manually set a color 
-preset [name] -> apply a built-in preset (blue, red, green, pink, bw, cyberpunk) 
-old -> re-apply the theme that was replaced by the current one 
-standard -> apply the standard theme

Print the current time with the given format 

Usage: 
$ time [index] (index means the index of the desired time_format

Open the Re:T-UI document editor. 

Usage: 
$ tuixt [textFile]

-rm -> uninstall Re:T-UI 
-about -> show info about the developer and the current version 
-reset -> removes the Re:T-UI folder and resets default settings (restart required) 
-log [fileName] -> log the text on screen to the file 
-priority [command] [int] -> set the priority of the selected command 
-sourcecode -> open the GitHub page 
-folder -> print the path of the Re:T-UI folder
-add [line] [text] -> add a new note at the specified line (the note will be appended if line is omitted) \n-rm [line] -> remove the note at the specified line \n-clear -> remove all the notes

Deprecated. Use search -ps [appName] to search the Play Store. 

Usage: 
$ search -ps [appName]

Send an HTTP POST request to a URL. 

Usage: 
$ post [url] [body] 

Example: 
$ post https://httpbin.org/post "hello world"

Manage and trigger webhooks. 

Usage: 
$ webhook -add [name] [url] [body_template] 
$ webhook -rm [name] 
$ webhook -ls 
$ webhook [name] [args...] 

Example: 
$ webhook -add myhook https://example.com/post "{"status":"%1"}" 
$ webhook myhook active
Toggle Wi-Fi on older Android versions, or open Wi-Fi settings on modern Android versions where direct toggles are restricted.

Make your device vibrate. 

Usage: 
$ vibrate [milliseconds] 
$ vibrate [pattern: wait, vibrate, wait, vibrate, wait, ...] 

Example: 
$ vibrate 300 
$ vibrate 0,300,500,300

-set [stream] [volume: 0-100] -> set the volume for the selected stream 
-profile [mode] -> set the profile mode 
-get [stream] -> get info about the selected stream 

stream: 
	* voice call -> 0 
	* system -> 1 
	* ringer -> 2 
	* media -> 3 
	* alarm -> 4 
	* notificaton -> 5 

mode: 
	* silent -> 0 
	* vibrate -> 1 
	* normal -> 2

- update -> perform a manual update (this won't work unless you set a custom weather key) 
-enable -> enable weather 
-disable -> disable weather 
-tutorial -> open the weather tutorial webpage 
-set_key [key] -> set a custom weather key 

Module: 
$ module -show weather 
$ module -dock add weather

Start or manage a timer. 

Usage: 
$ timer [duration] 
$ timer -add [duration] 
$ timer -stop 
$ timer -status 

Examples: 
$ timer 5m 
$ timer -add 30s

Start or manage a stopwatch. 

Usage: 
$ stopwatch 
$ stopwatch -stop 
$ stopwatch -reset 
$ stopwatch -status

-inc [appName] -> include an application 
-exc [appName] -> exclude an application 
-open -> open the Android notification shade 
-next -> show the next notification in the module 
-prev -> show the previous notification in the module 
-reply -> reply to the selected module notification 
-on -> add the notifications module to the dock 
-off -> remove the notifications module from the dock 
-color [color] [appName] -> set the color to be used for the application 
-format [ID] [appName] -> apply the format with the given ID to the corresponding application 
-add_filter [ID] [filter] -> create a new filter with the given ID 
-add_format [ID] [format] -> create a new format with the given ID 
-rm_filter [ID] -> delete the filter having the given ID 
-rm_format [ID] -> delete the format having the given ID 
-ls -> list included/excluded apps and content filters 
-access -> show the system settings page dedicated to the Notification Access 
-file -> open notifications.xml 
-tutorial -> show the tutorial page

-ls -> list your contacts 
-l [contactName] -> show details about a contact 
-add -> create a new contact 
-edit [contactName] -> edit an existing contact 
-rm [contactName] -> remove a contact
Close Re:T-UI and reset the default launcher

-add [ID] [update_time_in_seconds] [url] -> add a new RSS feed. Re:T-UI will try to get new contents every [update_time_in_seconds] seconds 
-rm [ID] -> remove this RSS feed 
-ls -> list your RSS feeds 
-l [ID] -> show the whole content of the corresponding RSS feed 
-show [ID] [boolean] -> toggle the [show] property of the given saved RSS feed 
-update_time [ID] [update_time_in_seconds] -> set the update time of the corresponding RSS feed 
-format [ID] [format] -> set the format of the corresponding RSS feed ([format] can be both a string or the ID of an existing format) 
-color [ID] [color] -> set the base color of the corresponding RSS feed 
-wifi_only [ID] [boolean] -> if [boolean] is true, the corresponding feed will be updated only if your device is connected to a WiFi network 
-last_check [ID] -> show the last time Re:T-UI checked this RSS feed 
-entry_tag [ID] -> set the base tag for every single entry (default: "item") 
-date_tag [ID] [tag] -> set the tag which holds info about the publication date (default: "pubDate") 
-add_command [ID] [IDs (comma separated)] [regex] [cmd] -> create a new regex command that will be applied on the RSS feeds corresponding to IDs 
-rm_command [ID] -> rm the corresponding regex command 
-frc [ID] -> update the corresponding RSS feed 
-info [ID] -> show info about the corresponding RSS feed settings 
-include_if_matches [ID] [regex] -> if the given regex matches on an RSS feed item, it will be shown ([regex] can be both a string or the ID of a regex saved inside regex.xml) 
-exclude_if_matches [ID] [regex] -> if the given regex matches on an RSS feed item, it won't be shown ([regex] can be both a string or the ID of a regex saved inside regex.xml) 
-add_format [ID] [format] -> create a new format that you will be able to use for more than one RSS feed 
-rm_format [ID] [format] -> remove an existing format 
-file -> open the file rss.xml 

Reddit/module example: 
$ rss -add 1 900 https://www.reddit.com/r/android/.rss 
$ rss -frc 1 
$ module -dock add rss 
$ module -show rss

Change Re:T-UI's current file path. 
Usage: 
$ cd [folder] 
$ cd .. 
$ cd /sdcard/Download

List files and folders in the current path or selected folder. 
Usage: 
$ ls 
$ ls [folder] 

For full browsing, use: 
$ files
Interrupt the current shell process and create a new one

Run an explicit embedded shell command. 
Usage: 
$ shell [command] 
Examples: 
$ shell pwd 
$ shell ls 
$ shell cd .. 
$ shell grep query file.txt 

For full Linux tooling, scripts, and packages, use Termux through termux/module commands.

Open a file. 
Usage: 
$ open [pathToFile] 

For interactive browsing, use: 
$ files

Dispatch Android intents from Re:T-UI. 
Usage: 
$ intent -view <uri> 
$ intent -activity -a <action> [-d <data>] [-t <mime>] [-p <package>] [-n <package/class>] [--es key value] [--ei key value] [--ez key true|false] 
$ intent -broadcast -a <action> [-p <package>] [-n <package/class>] [--es key value] 
$ intent -uri <intent-uri> 
$ intent -check ... 
Examples: 
$ intent -view geo:0,0?q=home 
$ intent -activity -a android.intent.action.SEND -t text/plain --es android.intent.extra.TEXT "standup notes" 
$ intent -broadcast -a net.dinglisch.android.tasker.ACTION_TASK -p net.dinglisch.android.taskerm --es task_name Work

Create or open the editable upcoming events Termux module. 
Usage: 
$ events 
$ events -access 
$ events -lookahead 3 
$ events -module 
$ module -add events termux:/data/data/com.termux/files/home/retui/events.sh 
$ module -refresh events 
$ module -show events
Change the username and device name: username [new_user] [new_device]
